title: Vacation & Secondary Home Mortgages in BC
metaTitle: Vacation Home Mortgage BC — Whistler, Sunshine Coast, Okanagan
metaDescription: Mortgage programs for vacation and secondary homes in Whistler, the Sunshine Coast, the Okanagan and across BC. Type A vs Type B properties explained.
intro: A second home in Whistler, the Sunshine Coast or the Okanagan — for family use, not rental — has its own mortgage rules. The big variable is whether the property is 'Type A' or 'Type B' under insurer guidelines.

body.heading: Type A vs Type B
paragraphs: Type A vacation properties are foundation-built, year-round residential properties with full kitchen and bathroom, on year-round road access, with year-round potable water and central heating. They qualify for insured mortgages up to 95% LTV (5% down) on owner-occupied secondary homes.

Type B properties are season-only or off-grid: woodstove heat, summer-only road access, lake-source water, no foundation. They typically require 25% down conventionally and don't qualify for insured high-ratio financing. Rates may also be slightly higher because the resale market is thinner.

body.heading: Whistler, Sunshine Coast, Okanagan — practical notes
paragraphs: Whistler condos and townhomes (Whistler Village, Creekside, Blackcomb): mostly Type A; 5–10% down is feasible on owner-occupied secondary purchases. Pemberton and Squamish: mostly Type A. Sunshine Coast (Gibsons, Sechelt, Roberts Creek): mixed; many properties are Type A but some hillside and waterfront homes are Type B. Okanagan (Kelowna, Penticton, Vernon, Osoyoos): mostly Type A in town; Type B in remote lake-shore properties.

If you plan to short-term rent the property (Airbnb / VRBO), the mortgage program changes — most insurers require strict owner-use and don't allow significant short-term rental. Confirm strata bylaws and municipal regulations before counting on rental income.

whoItsFor: Want a family vacation home in Whistler, the Okanagan, the Sunshine Coast or similar
Plan to use the property primarily yourself, not as an Airbnb
Have stable income to support carrying two properties

faqs.question: Can I rent the cabin out when I'm not there?
faqs.answer: Limited owner-friend-and-family use is typically fine. Active short-term rental income changes the mortgage classification — most insured products don't permit it. If short-term rental is the plan, we structure it as a rental property from day one.

faqs.question: Does my main home being mortgaged affect this?
faqs.answer: Yes — your existing mortgage payment, property tax and heating are part of your TDS calculation. We model the combined picture before applying.
